---
# Title/author/date are set on the custom title slide (first slide) below,
# built the same way as the Thank-You slide so the look is fully controlled.
pagetitle: "Matrix Decomposition Package for Metabolomics"

format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    css: styles.css

    # Slide geometry
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    margin: 0.05
    center: false

    # Navigation & behavior
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    controls: true
    hash: true
    preview-links: true
    incremental: false
    chalkboard: true
    code-line-numbers: true

    # Math rendering
    html-math-method: katex

    # Fonts & head includes (Montserrat = UTHSC's open-source brand font)
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
        <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Montserrat:wght@400;600;700;800;900&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">

    # Footer + Home button + Seal badge
    include-after-body:
      text: |
        <div class="uthsc-footer-bar">
          <div class="uthsc-left">
            <img src="assets/uthsc-logo-horizontal.png" alt="University of Tennessee Health Sciences">
          </div>
          <div class="uthsc-right">
            Department of Preventive Medicine / Biostatistics
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="uthsc-seal-badge">
          <img src="assets/uthsc-seal.png" alt="UTHSC College of Medicine seal">
        </div>

        <a class="home-btn" href="#outline" title="Go to Outline" aria-label="Go to Outline">
          <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" aria-hidden="true">
            <path d="M3 10.5L12 3l9 7.5V21a1 1 0 0 1-1 1h-5v-6H9v6H4a1 1 0 0 1-1-1z"/>
          </svg>
        </a>

resources:
  - styles.css
  - assets
---

## {.titlepage data-background-image="assets/uthsc-building-faded.png" data-background-size="cover" data-background-position="center" data-background-color="#ffffff"}

<div class="titlepage-slide">
<div class="titlepage-content">

<img class="tp-logo" src="assets/uthsc-logo-centered.png" alt="University of Tennessee Health Sciences">

<p class="tp-title">
  Julia Package for Large Matrix Decomposition in Metabolomics: BigRiverSchneiden
  <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" width="0.85em" height="0.95em" style="vertical-align:-0.08em;fill:#E78824;" aria-hidden="true">
    <path d="M9.64 7.64c.23-.5.36-1.05.36-1.64 0-2.21-1.79-4-4-4S2 3.79 2 6s1.79 4 4 4c.59 0 1.14-.13 1.64-.36L10 12l-2.36 2.36C7.14 14.13 6.59 14 6 14c-2.21 0-4 1.79-4 4s1.79 4 4 4 4-1.79 4-4c0-.59-.13-1.14-.36-1.64L12 14l7 7h3v-1L9.64 7.64zM6 8c-1.1 0-2-.89-2-2s.9-2 2-2 2 .89 2 2-.9 2-2 2zm0 12c-1.1 0-2-.89-2-2s.9-2 2-2 2 .89 2 2-.9 2-2 2zm6-7.5c-.28 0-.5-.22-.5-.5s.22-.5.5-.5.5.22.5.5-.22.5-.5.5zM19 3l-6 6 2 2 7-7V3z"/>
  </svg>
</p>
<p class="tp-author">Abhisek Banerjee</p>
<p class="tp-institute">UT Health Science Center — Preventive Medicine / Biostatistics</p>


</div>
</div>

## Outline {#outline}

<div class="toc-card">

1. [The data: metabolomics](#data)
2. [The challenge](#challenge)
3. [PCA — the main patterns](#pca)
4. [PLS — patterns tied to an outcome](#pls)
5. [Spin it yourself](#explore)
6. [The project](#project)

</div>


## The data: metabolomics {#data}

Metabolomics measures the thousands of small molecules called metabolites which the body produces through metabolism, giving a chemical snapshot of a person's state. Each sample (e.g. blood or urine) is profiled for thousands of metabolites, and stacking many samples gives one very large matrix with samples in rows, metabolites in columns.

```{ojs}
//----------------------------------------------------------------------
// SHARED SETUP (defined once, available to every slide in the deck)
//----------------------------------------------------------------------
d3 = require("d3@7")

makeRng = function (seed) {
  let s = seed >>> 0;
  return function () {
    s = (s + 0x6d2b79f5) >>> 0;
    let t = s;
    t = Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 15), 1 | t);
    t ^= t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), 61 | t);
    return ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296;
  };
}

randn = function (rng) {
  let u = 0, v = 0;
  while (u === 0) u = rng();
  while (v === 0) v = rng();
  return Math.sqrt(-2 * Math.log(u)) * Math.cos(2 * Math.PI * v);
}

// Two groups. Big spread along x (drives total variance) but the groups
// only differ along y (the thing we actually care about).
data = {
  const rng = makeRng(42);
  const pts = [];
  const N = 110;
  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) pts.push({ x: randn(rng) * 2.6, y:  1.3 + randn(rng) * 0.55, g: 0 });
  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) pts.push({ x: randn(rng) * 2.6, y: -1.3 + randn(rng) * 0.55, g: 1 });
  return pts;
}

meanVec = ({ x: d3.mean(data, d => d.x), y: d3.mean(data, d => d.y) })

// PCA direction = top eigenvector of the 2x2 covariance matrix.
pcaDir = {
  const mx = meanVec.x, my = meanVec.y;
  let a = 0, b = 0, c = 0;
  for (const d of data) { const dx = d.x - mx, dy = d.y - my; a += dx * dx; b += dx * dy; c += dy * dy; }
  const n = data.length; a /= n; b /= n; c /= n;
  const tr = a + c, det = a * c - b * b;
  const lam = tr / 2 + Math.sqrt(Math.max(0, (tr * tr) / 4 - det));
  const v1x = b, v1y = lam - a;
  const v2x = lam - c, v2y = b;
  let vx, vy;
  if (Math.hypot(v1x, v1y) >= Math.hypot(v2x, v2y)) { vx = v1x; vy = v1y; } else { vx = v2x; vy = v2y; }
  const nrm = Math.hypot(vx, vy) || 1;
  return { x: vx / nrm, y: vy / nrm };
}

// PLS direction (single outcome) = normalized covariance of features with the label.
plsDir = {
  const mx = meanVec.x, my = meanVec.y;
  let wx = 0, wy = 0;
  for (const d of data) { const yy = d.g === 0 ? 1 : -1; wx += (d.x - mx) * yy; wy += (d.y - my) * yy; }
  const nrm = Math.hypot(wx, wy) || 1;
  return { x: wx / nrm, y: wy / nrm };
}

kde = function (values, bw, xs) {
  return xs.map(x => {
    let s = 0;
    for (const v of values) { const u = (x - v) / bw; s += Math.exp(-0.5 * u * u); }
    return [x, s / (values.length * bw * Math.sqrt(2 * Math.PI))];
  });
}

// standardized separation between the two groups along a projection direction
separation = function (ts) {
  const t0 = ts.filter((_, i) => data[i].g === 0);
  const t1 = ts.filter((_, i) => data[i].g === 1);
  const m0 = d3.mean(t0), m1 = d3.mean(t1);
  const v0 = d3.variance(t0), v1 = d3.variance(t1);
  const sd = Math.sqrt((v0 + v1) / 2) || 1e-9;
  return Math.abs(m0 - m1) / sd;
}

// the drawing routine: takes ANY direction and renders scatter + projection
figure = function (dir, leftLabel) {
  const aid = "arrowhd-" + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2);
  const W = 1000, H = 380;
  const colors = ["#E78824", "#007E5A"];
  const names = ["Group A", "Group B"];

  const svg = d3.create("svg")
    .attr("viewBox", `0 0 ${W} ${H}`)
    .style("width", "100%").style("height", "auto")
    .style("font-family", "inherit");

  // ---- left: scatter (equal aspect so the arrow direction is honest) ----
  const sx0 = 30, sy0 = 40, sW = 560, sH = 300;
  const yhalf = (sH / sW) * 8.5;
  const sx = d3.scaleLinear().domain([-8.5, 8.5]).range([sx0, sx0 + sW]);
  const sy = d3.scaleLinear().domain([-yhalf, yhalf]).range([sy0 + sH, sy0]);

  svg.append("rect").attr("x", sx0).attr("y", sy0).attr("width", sW).attr("height", sH)
    .attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", "rgba(0,0,0,0.15)").attr("rx", 10);

  const mx = meanVec.x, my = meanVec.y;
  const ts = data.map(d => (d.x - mx) * dir.x + (d.y - my) * dir.y);
  const tmin = d3.min(ts), tmax = d3.max(ts);
  const tpad = (tmax - tmin) * 0.08 + 1e-6;
  const aLo = { x: mx + (tmin - tpad) * dir.x, y: my + (tmin - tpad) * dir.y };
  const aHi = { x: mx + (tmax + tpad) * dir.x, y: my + (tmax + tpad) * dir.y };

  data.forEach((d, i) => {
    const t = ts[i];
    svg.append("line")
      .attr("x1", sx(d.x)).attr("y1", sy(d.y))
      .attr("x2", sx(mx + t * dir.x)).attr("y2", sy(my + t * dir.y))
      .attr("stroke", colors[d.g]).attr("stroke-width", 1).attr("opacity", 0.12);
  });

  svg.append("defs").append("marker")
    .attr("id", aid).attr("viewBox", "0 0 10 10")
    .attr("refX", 8).attr("refY", 5).attr("markerWidth", 7).attr("markerHeight", 7)
    .attr("orient", "auto-start-reverse")
    .append("path").attr("d", "M0,0 L10,5 L0,10 z").attr("fill", "#2b2b2b");
  svg.append("line")
    .attr("x1", sx(aLo.x)).attr("y1", sy(aLo.y))
    .attr("x2", sx(aHi.x)).attr("y2", sy(aHi.y))
    .attr("stroke", "#2b2b2b").attr("stroke-width", 2.5).attr("marker-end", `url(#${aid})`);

  data.forEach((d, i) => {
    const t = ts[i];
    svg.append("circle").attr("cx", sx(mx + t * dir.x)).attr("cy", sy(my + t * dir.y))
      .attr("r", 3).attr("fill", colors[d.g]).attr("opacity", 0.55);
  });

  data.forEach(d => {
    svg.append("circle").attr("cx", sx(d.x)).attr("cy", sy(d.y)).attr("r", 4.5)
      .attr("fill", colors[d.g]).attr("opacity", 0.9)
      .attr("stroke", "white").attr("stroke-width", 0.8);
  });

  svg.append("text").attr("x", sx0).attr("y", 26).attr("font-size", 15).attr("font-weight", 700).attr("fill", "#333")
    .text(leftLabel);

  const lg = svg.append("g").attr("transform", `translate(${sx0 + sW - 150},${sy0 + 12})`);
  names.forEach((nm, i) => {
    lg.append("circle").attr("cx", 0).attr("cy", i * 20).attr("r", 6).attr("fill", colors[i]);
    lg.append("text").attr("x", 12).attr("y", i * 20 + 4).attr("font-size", 13).attr("fill", "#333").text(nm);
  });

  // ---- right: distribution after projecting ----
  const px0 = 640, py0 = 40, pW = 330, pH = 300;
  svg.append("text").attr("x", px0).attr("y", 26).attr("font-size", 15).attr("font-weight", 700).attr("fill", "#333")
    .text("After projecting onto that line");

  const tsc = d3.scaleLinear().domain([tmin - tpad, tmax + tpad]).range([px0, px0 + pW]);
  svg.append("line").attr("x1", px0).attr("y1", py0 + pH).attr("x2", px0 + pW).attr("y2", py0 + pH)
    .attr("stroke", "rgba(0,0,0,0.3)").attr("stroke-width", 1.5);

  const bw = 0.45 * (d3.deviation(ts) || 1);
  const grid = d3.range(60).map(k => (tmin - tpad) + (k / 59) * ((tmax + tpad) - (tmin - tpad)));
  const g0 = kde(ts.filter((_, i) => data[i].g === 0), bw, grid);
  const g1 = kde(ts.filter((_, i) => data[i].g === 1), bw, grid);
  const maxd = d3.max([...g0, ...g1], d => d[1]) || 1;
  const dsc = d3.scaleLinear().domain([0, maxd]).range([py0 + pH, py0 + 44]);

  const area = d3.area().x(d => tsc(d[0])).y0(py0 + pH).y1(d => dsc(d[1])).curve(d3.curveBasis);
  svg.append("path").attr("d", area(g0)).attr("fill", colors[0]).attr("opacity", 0.45);
  svg.append("path").attr("d", area(g1)).attr("fill", colors[1]).attr("opacity", 0.45);

  const lineg = d3.line().x(d => tsc(d[0])).y(d => dsc(d[1])).curve(d3.curveBasis);
  svg.append("path").attr("d", lineg(g0)).attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", colors[0]).attr("stroke-width", 2);
  svg.append("path").attr("d", lineg(g1)).attr("fill", "none").attr("stroke", colors[1]).attr("stroke-width", 2);

  data.forEach((d, i) => {
    svg.append("line").attr("x1", tsc(ts[i])).attr("y1", py0 + pH).attr("x2", tsc(ts[i])).attr("y2", py0 + pH - 7)
      .attr("stroke", colors[d.g]).attr("stroke-width", 1).attr("opacity", 0.5);
  });

  // live separation readout
  const sep = separation(ts);
  svg.append("text").attr("x", px0 + pW).attr("y", py0 + 16).attr("text-anchor", "end")
    .attr("font-size", 14).attr("font-weight", 800)
    .attr("fill", sep > 1.2 ? "#007E5A" : "#b06a1f")
    .text(`Group separation: ${sep.toFixed(2)}`);

  return svg.node();
}

panel = function (method) {
  return method === "pca"
    ? figure(pcaDir, "PC₁ direction (most variation)")
    : figure(plsDir, "PLS₁ direction (linked to outcome)");
}
```

```{ojs}
heatmap = {
  const cols = 44, rows = 16;
  const rng = makeRng(7);
  const W = 920, H = 250, pad = { t: 34, r: 20, b: 24, l: 60 };
  const cw = (W - pad.l - pad.r) / cols, ch = (H - pad.t - pad.b) / rows;
  const color = d3.scaleSequential(d3.interpolateOranges).domain([0, 1]);

  const svg = d3.create("svg").attr("viewBox", `0 0 ${W} ${H}`)
    .style("width", "100%").style("height", "auto").style("font-family", "inherit");

  for (let i = 0; i < rows; i++)
    for (let j = 0; j < cols; j++)
      svg.append("rect")
        .attr("x", pad.l + j * cw).attr("y", pad.t + i * ch)
        .attr("width", cw - 1).attr("height", ch - 1).attr("rx", 1)
        .attr("fill", color(Math.pow(rng(), 1.4)));

  svg.append("text").attr("x", pad.l).attr("y", 22).attr("font-weight", 700).attr("font-size", 15).attr("fill", "#333")
    .text("metabolites  (thousands of columns →)");
  const cy = pad.t + (H - pad.t - pad.b) / 2;
  svg.append("text").attr("x", 16).attr("y", cy).attr("transform", `rotate(-90 16 ${cy})`)
    .attr("text-anchor", "middle").attr("font-weight", 700).attr("font-size", 15).attr("fill", "#333")
    .text("samples ↓");

  return svg.node();
}
```


## The challenge {#challenge}

A table with thousands of columns is very difficult to read directly, we just can't eyeball patterns across that many measurements.

We want to squeeze thousands of numbers into a few meaningful directions that still capture what's going on. We will perform this using matrix decomposition.

<br>

```{ojs}
// ---- definitions (named cells render nothing) ----
decomp = {
  const n = 24, p = 56, R = 6;
  const rng = (() => { let s = 11 >>> 0; return () => { s = (s + 0x6d2b79f5) >>> 0; let t = s; t = Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 15), 1 | t); t ^= t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), 61 | t); return ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296; }; })();
  const s = [9, 6, 4.5, 3, 2, 1.2];
  const U = [], V = [];
  for (let r = 0; r < R; r++) {
    const ph = rng() * Math.PI * 2, ph2 = rng() * Math.PI * 2;
    const ur = [], vr = [];
    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) ur.push(Math.cos(Math.PI * (r + 1) * i / n + ph));
    for (let j = 0; j < p; j++) vr.push(Math.cos(Math.PI * (r + 1) * j / (p / 3) + ph2));
    const nu = Math.hypot(...ur) || 1, nv = Math.hypot(...vr) || 1;
    U.push(ur.map(x => x / nu)); V.push(vr.map(x => x / nv));
  }
  const noise = [];
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) { const row = []; for (let j = 0; j < p; j++) row.push(0.04 * (rng() * 2 - 1)); noise.push(row); }
  const M = [];
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) { const row = []; for (let j = 0; j < p; j++) { let v = noise[i][j]; for (let r = 0; r < R; r++) v += s[r] * U[r][i] * V[r][j]; row.push(v); } M.push(row); }
  const maxAbs = d3.max(M.flat(), v => Math.abs(v));
  const uMax = d3.max(U.flat(), v => Math.abs(v)) || 1;
  const vMax = d3.max(V.flat(), v => Math.abs(v)) || 1;
  const totalEnergy = s.reduce((a, b) => a + b * b, 0) + d3.sum(noise.flat(), v => v * v);
  return { n, p, R, s, U, V, M, maxAbs, uMax, vMax, totalEnergy };
}

recon = function (k) {
  const { n, p, U, V, s } = decomp;
  const out = [];
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) { const row = []; for (let j = 0; j < p; j++) { let v = 0; for (let r = 0; r < k; r++) v += s[r] * U[r][i] * V[r][j]; row.push(v); } out.push(row); }
  return out;
}

decompColor = d3.scaleSequential(d3.interpolateRgbBasis(["#0a7d63", "#f4f1ec", "#E78824"])).domain([-decomp.maxAbs, decomp.maxAbs])
factorColor = d3.scaleSequential(d3.interpolateRgbBasis(["#0a7d63", "#f4f1ec", "#E78824"])).domain([-1, 1])

drawDecomp = function (k) {
  const { n, p, M, U, V, s, uMax, vMax, totalEnergy } = decomp;
  const cw = 5, W = 1000, H = 240;
  const Mk = recon(k);
  const svg = d3.create("svg").attr("viewBox", `0 0 ${W} ${H}`)
    .style("width", "100%").style("height", "auto").style("font-family", "inherit");

  const block = (get, rows, cols, x, y, color) => {
    for (let i = 0; i < rows; i++) for (let j = 0; j < cols; j++)
      svg.append("rect").attr("x", x + j * cw).attr("y", y + i * cw)
        .attr("width", cw - 0.4).attr("height", cw - 0.4).attr("fill", color(get(i, j)));
  };
  const txt = (t, x, y, o = {}) => svg.append("text").attr("x", x).attr("y", y)
    .attr("font-size", o.size || 13).attr("font-weight", o.w || 700)
    .attr("fill", o.fill || "#333").attr("text-anchor", o.anchor || "start").text(t);

  const y0 = 44;
  txt("M  (n × p)", 10, y0 - 8);
  block((i, j) => M[i][j], n, p, 10, y0, decompColor);

  txt("≈", 10 + p * cw + 12, y0 + n * cw / 2 + 6, { size: 24, w: 800, anchor: "middle" });

  const xU = 10 + p * cw + 34;
  txt("U", xU, y0 - 8);
  block((i, r) => U[r][i] / uMax, n, k, xU, y0, factorColor);

  const xMul = xU + k * cw + 8;
  txt("×", xMul, y0 + n * cw / 2 + 5, { size: 18, w: 800, anchor: "middle" });

  const xV = xMul + 14;
  txt("V", xV, y0 - 8);
  block((r, j) => V[r][j] / vMax, k, p, xV, y0, factorColor);

  const xArr = xV + p * cw + 14;
  txt("→", xArr, y0 + n * cw / 2 + 6, { size: 22, w: 800, anchor: "middle" });

  const xR = xArr + 18;
  txt(`Rebuilt (k = ${k})`, xR, y0 - 8);
  block((i, j) => Mk[i][j], n, p, xR, y0, decompColor);

  const pct = (s.slice(0, k).reduce((a, b) => a + b * b, 0) / totalEnergy) * 100;
  const bx = 10, by = y0 + n * cw + 26, bw = 520, bh = 20;
  txt("Variation captured", bx, by - 6);
  svg.append("rect").attr("x", bx).attr("y", by).attr("width", bw).attr("height", bh).attr("rx", 5).attr("fill", "#eee");
  svg.append("rect").attr("x", bx).attr("y", by).attr("width", bw * pct / 100).attr("height", bh).attr("rx", 5).attr("fill", "#E78824");
  txt(`${pct.toFixed(0)}%`, bx + bw + 10, by + bh - 3, { w: 800, size: 16, fill: "#E78824" });

  const full = n * p, comp = k * (n + p);
  txt(`Stored: k·(n+p) = ${comp}  vs  n·p = ${full}   (${(comp / full * 100).toFixed(0)}% of the original — real p is in the thousands)`,
    bx, by + bh + 20, { size: 12, w: 600, fill: "#777" });

  return svg.node();
}

// ---- compact one-row control (own font size, no wrapping) ----
viewof krank = {
  const max = decomp.R;
  const cont = html`<div style="display:flex;align-items:center;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:14px;font-size:18px;font-family:inherit;margin:4px 0 6px;"></div>`;
  const range = html`<input type="range" min="1" max="${max}" step="1" value="1" style="width:240px;accent-color:#E78824;">`;
  const val = html`<span style="font-weight:800;color:#E78824;min-width:1.2em;">1</span>`;
  const sweep = html`<button style="padding:6px 14px;border:0;border-radius:8px;color:#fff;font-weight:700;cursor:pointer;font-family:inherit;font-size:15px;background:#007E5A;">▶ Add one by one</button>`;
  const set = v => { range.value = v; val.textContent = v; cont.value = +v; cont.dispatchEvent(new Event("input", { bubbles: true })); };
  range.oninput = () => set(range.value);
  sweep.onclick = () => { let k = 1; set(1); const id = setInterval(() => { k++; if (k > max) { clearInterval(id); return; } set(k); }, 450); };
  cont.value = 1;
  cont.append(html`<span style="font-weight:700;white-space:nowrap;">Components (k):</span>`, range, val, sweep);
  return cont;
}

// ---- the figure (capped width so it can't blow up on wide screens) ----
html`<div style="max-width:1040px;margin:6px auto 0;">${drawDecomp(krank)}</div>`
```



## PCA — the main patterns {#pca}

PCA looks at the data table on its own and asks: along which direction do the samples vary the most? That direction (**PC₁**) often reveals natural groupings — clans or subclasses — even though it was never told they exist.

```{ojs}
panel("pca")
```

*PCA is **unsupervised**. The biggest source of variation isn't always what you care about — here the groups stay mixed, and the separation score is near zero.*


## PLS — patterns tied to an outcome {#pls}

PLS uses the data table and an outcome (e.g. disease vs. healthy). It looks for directions that vary across samples and track that outcome so the groups we want are cleanly apart. 

 Same data, but the outcome guides the direction hence the separation score jumps up.

```{ojs}
panel("pls")
```




## Spin it yourself {#explore}

Drag the angle, or hit a button to animate the projection line to what PCA vs. PLS would choose. Watch the separation score (top-right) rise and fall.

```{ojs}
viewof theta = {
  const toDeg = v => ((Math.atan2(v.y, v.x) * 180 / Math.PI) % 180 + 180) % 180;
  const pcaA = toDeg(pcaDir);
  const plsA = toDeg(plsDir);

  const cont = html`<div style="display:flex;align-items:center;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:14px;font-size:18px;font-family:inherit;margin:4px 0 8px;"></div>`;
  const range = html`<input type="range" min="0" max="180" step="0.5" value="0" style="width:240px;accent-color:#E78824;">`;
  const val = html`<span style="font-weight:800;color:#E78824;min-width:2.8em;">0°</span>`;
  const bStyle = "padding:6px 14px;border:0;border-radius:8px;color:#fff;font-weight:700;cursor:pointer;font-family:inherit;font-size:15px;";
  const bPCA = html`<button style="${bStyle}background:#E78824;">▶ to PCA</button>`;
  const bPLS = html`<button style="${bStyle}background:#007E5A;">▶ to PLS</button>`;

  const set = v => {
    range.value = v;
    val.textContent = (+v).toFixed(0) + "°";
    cont.value = +v;
    cont.dispatchEvent(new Event("input", { bubbles: true }));
  };
  range.oninput = () => set(range.value);

  let raf;
  const animateTo = target => {
    cancelAnimationFrame(raf);
    const start = +cont.value, t0 = performance.now(), dur = 750;
    const step = now => {
      const k = Math.min(1, (now - t0) / dur);
      const e = k < 0.5 ? 2 * k * k : 1 - Math.pow(-2 * k + 2, 2) / 2;
      set(start + (target - start) * e);
      if (k < 1) raf = requestAnimationFrame(step);
    };
    raf = requestAnimationFrame(step);
  };
  bPCA.onclick = () => animateTo(pcaA);
  bPLS.onclick = () => animateTo(plsA);

  cont.value = 0;
  cont.append(
    html`<span style="font-weight:700;white-space:nowrap;">Projection angle:</span>`,
    range, val, bPCA, bPLS
  );
  return cont;
}
```

```{ojs}
{
  const th = theta * Math.PI / 180;
  const fig = figure({ x: Math.cos(th), y: Math.sin(th) }, "Your chosen projection");
  return html`<div style="max-width:1040px;margin:6px auto 0;">${fig}</div>`;
}
```




## The project {#project}


- Build a Julia package implementing PCA, PLS, PMD, and related decompositions.
- Engineered for the very large matrices typical for metabolomics having thousands of metabolites across many samples.
- A clean, consistent light package meant to be a shared foundation for further biostatistics packages in the department.
- Why Julia: near-C performance with readable high-level.


<br>

<div class="uthsc-green-box">

The aim: a fast, reusable decomposition toolkit
that other department projects can build on.

</div>



# Joint & Individual Variation Explained (JIVE) {#jive}

*Lock, Hoadley, Marron & Nobel (2013)* 

# The big idea {#jive-intuition}
<style>
#jive-intuition form,
#jive-intuition form * { font-size: 14px !important; line-height: 1.2 !important; font-weight: 500 !important; }
#jive-intuition form label { display: block; margin-bottom: 3px; }
#jive-intuition form input[type=range] { height: 16px !important; width: 260px !important; vertical-align: middle; }
#jive-intuition form input[type=number] { width: 3.4em !important; height: 1.5em !important; padding: 0 3px !important; }
</style>
The same patients take **two different tests**. Each dot is one patient: blood test (→) vs gene test (↑).

::: columns
::: column
**Shared** signal: patients line up on the line — both tests say the same thing.
:::
::: column
**Separate** signal: a shapeless cloud — each test sees something different.
:::
:::

```{ojs}
viewof intuShared = Inputs.range([0, 1], {step: 0.01, value: 0.85, label: "How much the two tests SHARE"})
```

```{ojs}
intuFig = {
  function mul(a){return function(){a|=0;a=a+0x6D2B79F5|0;let t=Math.imul(a^a>>>15,1|a);t=t+Math.imul(t^t>>>7,61|t)^t;return((t^t>>>14)>>>0)/4294967296;};}
  const rng = mul(11);
  const randn = () => { let u=0,v=0; while(!u)u=rng(); while(!v)v=rng(); return Math.sqrt(-2*Math.log(u))*Math.cos(2*Math.PI*v); };
  const n = 80;
  const s = d3.range(n).map(()=>randn());
  const wS = 2.4*intuShared, wI = 2.2*(1-intuShared);
  const x = d3.range(n).map(i => wS*s[i] + wI*randn());
  const y = d3.range(n).map(i => wS*s[i] + wI*randn());

  const W=560,H=280,pad=44,e=3;
  const xs=d3.scaleLinear().domain([-e,e]).range([pad,W-pad]);
  const ys=d3.scaleLinear().domain([-e,e]).range([H-pad,pad]);
  const svg=d3.create("svg").attr("viewBox",`0 0 ${W} ${H}`)
    .style("width","100%").style("max-width","540px").style("height","auto").style("display","block").style("margin","0 auto");

  svg.append("line").attr("x1",xs(-e)).attr("y1",ys(-e)).attr("x2",xs(e)).attr("y2",ys(e))
     .attr("stroke","#C14C23").attr("stroke-dasharray","6 5").attr("stroke-width",1.5).attr("opacity",0.8);

  svg.selectAll("circle").data(d3.range(n)).join("circle")
    .attr("cx",i=>xs(Math.max(-e,Math.min(e,x[i])))).attr("cy",i=>ys(Math.max(-e,Math.min(e,y[i]))))
    .attr("r",5).attr("fill","#E78824").attr("opacity",0.8).attr("stroke","#fff").attr("stroke-width",0.6);

  svg.append("text").attr("x",W/2).attr("y",H-12).attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("font-size",13).attr("fill","#333").text("Blood test  →");
  svg.append("text").attr("transform",`translate(15,${H/2}) rotate(-90)`).attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("font-size",13).attr("fill","#333").text("Gene test  →");
  return svg.node();
}
```

<div class="uthsc-orange-box" style="font-size:0.82em; padding:8px 14px;">
JIVE pulls apart the part the tests **share** (the line) from the part each sees **alone** (the spread).
</div>



# Setup & model {#jive-model}

Each data type is features $\times$ samples, sharing the **same $n$ samples (columns)**:

$$X_i \in \mathbb{R}^{p_i\times n},\qquad X=\begin{bmatrix}X_1\\\vdots\\X_k\end{bmatrix}$$

Decompose every type into three pieces:

$$X_i \;=\; \underbrace{J_i}_{\text{joint}} \;+\; \underbrace{A_i}_{\text{individual}} \;+\; \underbrace{\varepsilon_i}_{\text{noise}}$$


- **Joint** $J=[J_1;\dots;J_k]$ has rank $r$: a *single shared sample-pattern* present in every type
- **Individual** $A_i$ has rank $r_i$: structure only type $i$ sees
- **Orthogonalitys**, $\;J\,A_i^{\top}=0$: this makes the split **identifiable** — neither piece can absorb the other's variation



# The algorithm: alternate two SVDs {#jive-algo}
<style>
#jive-algo .algo-btns { display:flex; gap:10px; margin:6px 0 4px; flex-wrap:wrap; }
#jive-algo .algo-btns button {
  font-size:15px; font-weight:700; padding:7px 16px; border-radius:10px;
  border:2px solid #cfcfcf; background:#fbfbfa; color:#2a2a2a; cursor:pointer;
  font-family:inherit; transition:all .12s ease;
}
#jive-algo .algo-btns button:hover { border-color:#E78824; }
#jive-algo .algo-btns button.on { border-color:#C14C23; background:#fff2e6; color:#C14C23; box-shadow:0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.12); }
</style>
Hold the ranks $r, r_1,\dots,r_k$ fixed and loop until the fit stops changing.

```{ojs}
viewof algoStep = {
  const labels = ["① Joint step", "② Individual step", "③ Refresh"];
  const wrap = html`<div class="algo-btns"></div>`;
  let value = 0;
  const buttons = labels.map((t,i) => {
    const b = html`<button>${t}</button>`;
    b.onclick = () => { value = i; wrap.value = i; render(); wrap.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input")); };
    wrap.append(b);
    return b;
  });
  const render = () => buttons.forEach((b,i)=> b.classList.toggle("on", i===value));
  render();
  wrap.value = value;
  return wrap;
}
```

```{ojs}
algoFig = {
  const step = algoStep;                       // 0 joint, 1 individual, 2 refresh
  const W = 940, H = 380;
  const O = "#E78824", Od = "#C14C23", ink = "#2a2a2a", mut = "#9a9a9a";
  const svg = d3.create("svg").attr("viewBox",`0 0 ${W} ${H}`)
    .style("width","100%").style("max-width","960px").style("height","auto").style("display","block").style("margin","0 auto");

  const defs = svg.append("defs");
  const arrow = (id,color)=>{ const m=defs.append("marker").attr("id",id).attr("viewBox","0 0 10 10").attr("refX",8).attr("refY",5).attr("markerWidth",7).attr("markerHeight",7).attr("orient","auto-start-reverse"); m.append("path").attr("d","M0,0 L10,5 L0,10 z").attr("fill",color); };
  arrow("aOn",Od); arrow("aOff",mut);

  const active = i => step===i;
  const box = (x,y,w,h,i,title,bodyLines)=>{
    const g = svg.append("g");
    g.append("rect").attr("x",x).attr("y",y).attr("width",w).attr("height",h).attr("rx",14)
      .attr("fill", active(i)? "#fff7ef":"#fbfbfa").attr("stroke", active(i)? Od:"#cfcfcf").attr("stroke-width", active(i)?3:1.4);
    g.append("rect").attr("x",x).attr("y",y).attr("width",w).attr("height",5).attr("rx",2.5).attr("fill", active(i)? O:"#e3e3e3");
    g.append("text").attr("x",x+w/2).attr("y",y+30).attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("font-size",17).attr("font-weight",800).attr("fill", active(i)? Od:ink).text(title);
    bodyLines.forEach((ln,k)=> g.append("text").attr("x",x+w/2).attr("y",y+54+k*22).attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("font-size",13.5).attr("fill", active(i)? ink:mut).html(ln));
  };

  const y0=70, bh=132, bw=270;
  const x1=20, x2=(W-bw)/2, x3=W-bw-20;
  box(x1,y0,bw,bh,0,"① Joint step",      ["Best rank-<tspan font-style='italic'>r</tspan> SVD of stacked data","J = U \u039B V\u1D40","V = shared sample scores"]);
  box(x2,y0,bw,bh,1,"② Individual step", ["For each type i, fit what's left","off the joint space:","Aᵢ = rankᵢ SVD of (Xᵢ−Jᵢ)(I−VV\u1D40)"]);
  box(x3,y0,bw,bh,2,"③ Refresh",         ["Remove individual part","joint target ← Xᵢ − Aᵢ","feed back & repeat"]);

  const midY = y0+bh/2;
  const fwd = (xa,xb,i)=> svg.append("line").attr("x1",xa).attr("x2",xb).attr("y1",midY).attr("y2",midY)
      .attr("stroke", active(i)? Od:mut).attr("stroke-width", active(i)?3:1.6).attr("marker-end","url(#"+(active(i)?"aOn":"aOff")+")");
  fwd(x1+bw, x2, 0);
  fwd(x2+bw, x3, 1);

  const loopActive = active(2), ly = y0+bh+54;
  svg.append("path").attr("d", `M ${x3+bw/2} ${y0+bh} V ${ly} H ${x1+bw/2} V ${y0+bh}`)
    .attr("fill","none").attr("stroke", loopActive? Od:mut).attr("stroke-width", loopActive?3:1.6)
    .attr("stroke-dasharray", loopActive? "none":"5 5").attr("marker-end","url(#"+(loopActive?"aOn":"aOff")+")");
  svg.append("text").attr("x",(x1+x3+bw)/2).attr("y",ly+18).attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("font-size",13).attr("font-style","italic").attr("fill", loopActive?Od:mut).text("loop until the fit stops changing");

  const caps = [
    "Pull out the structure all data types share.",
    "I − VV\u1D40 removes shared directions \u2192 keeps J A\u1D62\u1D40 = 0 (joint \u22A5 individual).",
    "Subtract individual structure, then re-estimate the joint part."
  ];
  svg.append("text").attr("x",W/2).attr("y",36).attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("font-size",15).attr("font-weight",700).attr("fill",Od).text(caps[step]);

  return svg.node();
}
```
Every SVD is the **best** low-rank fit (Eckart–Young), so each pass can only **lower the residual**; the projection $I-VV^{\top}$ keeps joint and individual from stealing each other's variation.


# Relation to PCA {#jive-pca}

PCA approximates one matrix as **scores × loadings**, $\;X \approx US$ ($S$: $r\times n$ sample scores, $U$: $p\times r$ loadings).

JIVE keeps that form, but with **one shared score block** plus a **private block per data type**:

$$X_i \;=\; \underbrace{U_i\,S}_{\text{joint}} \;+\; \underbrace{W_i\,S_i}_{\text{individual}} \;+\; R_i$$


- $S$ — **joint scores** ($r\times n$): sample patterns shared by *every* data type; $U_i$ expresses them in type $i$'s variables
- $S_i$ — **individual scores** ($r_i\times n$): sample patterns seen by type $i$ *alone*, with loadings $W_i$
- Set every $S_i=0$ and one block → you're back to ordinary PCA


<div class="uthsc-green-box">
JIVE = PCA with a **single common score matrix $S$ across all data types**, plus separate scores $S_i$ for each.
</div>



# Part 2 — Making it fast {#opt}

I took my existing decomposition functions (PCA, PMD, PLS, sPLS‑DA, JIVE) and optimized the hot loops — same math, same results, far fewer allocations.

# How I optimized {#opt-method}

<style>
#opt-method .opt-chips { display:flex; gap:7px; flex-wrap:wrap; margin-bottom:12px; }
#opt-method .opt-chip { font:700 12.5px inherit; padding:5px 11px; border-radius:20px; cursor:pointer;
  border:1.5px solid #ddd; background:#fff; color:#555; transition:all .12s; }
#opt-method .opt-chip:hover { border-color:#E78824; color:#C14C23; }
#opt-method .opt-chip.on { background:#E78824; border-color:#E78824; color:#fff; }
#opt-method pre { margin:0; }
</style>

```{ojs}
optFrames = [
  {
    tag: "#1 Preallocate", title: "Allocate buffers once, reuse them",
    body: "Work arrays built inside a loop are re-allocated every pass. Create them once, outside, and overwrite in place.",
    bad: `for it in 1:iters\n    uh = Vector{Float64}(undef, p)   # new array each pass\n    ...\nend`,
    good: `uh = Vector{Float64}(undef, p)       # once\nfor it in 1:iters\n    # reuse uh every pass\nend`,
    win: "Foundation of every function — removes repeated loop allocations."
  },
  {
    tag: "#2 mul!", title: "In-place matrix multiply",
    body: "C = A*B allocates a fresh C each call. mul!(C,A,B) writes into an existing buffer.",
    bad: `tY   = Ry * vh        # allocates\nuraw = R' * tY        # allocates`,
    good: `mul!(tY, Ry, vh)              # tY = Ry*vh\nmul!(uraw, transpose(R), tY)  # no temporaries`,
    win: "Removes the per-iteration matmul allocations."
  },
  {
    tag: "#3 Fused mul!", title: "Fused multiply–add",
    body: "mul!(C,A,B,α,β) computes C = α·A·B + β·C in one call — subtract a product in place, no temporary.",
    bad: `C = C - A*B           # allocates A*B`,
    good: `mul!(C, A, B, -1.0, 1.0)     # C = C - A*B, in place`,
    win: "Eliminates temporaries in accumulate/subtract steps."
  },
  {
    tag: "#4 BLAS.ger!", title: "Rank-1 update without forming a matrix",
    body: "Deflation R = R − t·p' builds a whole n×p matrix. ger! applies the rank-1 update in place.",
    bad: `R = R - tX * pX'      # forms n×p matrix`,
    good: `BLAS.ger!(-1.0, tX, pX, R)   # R -= tX*pX'`,
    win: "PMD: ~7000× fewer allocations, 340× less memory, 1.5× faster."
  },
  {
    tag: "#5 syrk!", title: "Symmetric products",
    body: "X'X is symmetric — syrk! computes only one triangle instead of the full product.",
    bad: `C = X' * X            # full product`,
    good: `BLAS.syrk!('U','T', 1.0, X, 0.0, C)  # half the work`,
    win: "Cheaper Gram matrices where X'X appears."
  },
  {
    tag: "#6 @view", title: "Reference slices, don't copy",
    body: "X[:, i] copies the column into a new array; @view points at the same memory, so no copy is made.",
    bad: `col = X[:, i]              # copies the column\nuh  = F.U[:, 1]            # copies again`,
    good: `col = @view X[:, i]        # references, no copy\ncopyto!(uh, @view F.U[:, 1])  # write into uh, no copy`,
    win: "Stops silent copies on every slice."
  },
  {
    tag: "#7 In-place bcast", title: "Broadcast into existing arrays",
    body: "Broadcasting with new bindings allocates;  @. write into the array you already have.",
    bad: `absx = abs.(x)`,
    good: `@. absx = abs(x)`,
    win: "Centering, normalizing, abs — all allocation-free."
  },
  {
    tag: "#8 svd!", title: "In-place SVD",
    body: "svd!(A) overwrites A instead of copying it. Keep a robust fallback for degenerate matrices.",
    bad: `F = svd(A)            # copies A`,
    good: `F = svd!(A)                  # overwrites A\n# fallback: svd!(A; alg=QRIteration())`,
    win: "Saves a full copy of the (often large) input."
  },
  {
    tag: "#9 Algebra (JIVE)", title: "Never form a big projector",
    body: "The orthogonality step needs (I − VVᵀ). Forming the n×n projector is the memory blowup; associativity avoids it.",
    bad: `tmp * (I - V*V')      # forms n×n projector`,
    good: `tmp .-= (tmp*V)*V'    # same result, small intermediates`,
    win: "JIVE: 871 → 277 MiB. The single biggest win."
  },
  {
    tag: "#10 Reductions", title: "Allocation-free convergence checks",
    body: "‖a−b‖² written as sum(abs2, a .- b) allocates the difference each check. A manual @simd loop doesn't.",
    bad: `delta = sum(abs2, a .- b)   # allocates a .- b`,
    good: `s = 0.0\n@inbounds @simd for i in eachindex(a)\n    d = a[i]-b[i]; s += d*d\nend`,
    win: "Hot convergence checks stop allocating entirely."
  },
  {
    tag: "#11 sortperm!", title: "In-place sort",
    body: "sortperm(x) allocates the index vector; sortperm!(ord, x) reuses a preallocated one.",
    bad: `ord = sortperm(absx)        # allocates`,
    good: `sortperm!(ord, absx)        # ord preallocated`,
    win: "No allocation in ranking/threshold steps."
  },
{
  tag: "#15 Thin SVD", title: "Compute only the vectors you need",
  body: "A full SVD builds every singular vector; PCA/JIVE use only the leading ones. The thin form computes just those and skips the rest.",
  bad:  `F = svd(A)             # full: all singular vectors`,
  good: `F = svd(A; full=false) # thin: only the leading vectors`,
  win:  "Less compute and memory whenever you only need the top components."
}
];
```

```{ojs}
viewof optStep = {
  const wrap = html`<div></div>`;
  const chips = html`<div class="opt-chips"></div>`;
  const els = optFrames.map((f,k)=>{
    const c = html`<span class="opt-chip">${f.tag}</span>`;
    c.onclick = () => { wrap.value=k; render(); wrap.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input")); };
    return c;
  });
  chips.append(...els);
  const render = () => els.forEach((c,k)=>c.classList.toggle("on", k===wrap.value));
  wrap.append(chips);
  wrap.value = 0; render();
  return wrap;
}
```

```{ojs}
optView = {
  const f = optFrames[optStep];
  const Od="#C14C23", G="#4D701F", ink="#2a2a2a", O="#E78824";
  const card = html`<div style="border:2px solid #eee;border-top:5px solid ${O};border-radius:14px;padding:14px 20px;background:#fff;min-height:280px"></div>`;
  card.append(html`<div style="font:800 22px inherit;color:${Od};margin-bottom:4px">${f.title}</div>`);
  card.append(html`<div style="font:400 15.5px inherit;color:${ink};margin-bottom:12px">${f.body}</div>`);

  const grid = html`<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:14px"></div>`;
  const codebox = (label,code,color)=>{
    const b = html`<div></div>`;
    b.append(html`<div style="font:700 12px inherit;color:${color};margin-bottom:4px">${label}</div>`);
    const pre = html`<pre style="background:#f6f6f4;border-left:4px solid ${color};border-radius:6px;padding:10px 12px;font:500 13px ui-monospace,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;white-space:pre;overflow:auto"></pre>`;
    pre.textContent = code; b.append(pre); return b;
  };
  grid.append(codebox("BEFORE", f.bad, "#b0b0b0"));
  grid.append(codebox("AFTER", f.good, G));
  card.append(grid);

  card.append(html`<div style="margin-top:12px;background:#eef3e8;border-radius:8px;padding:8px 14px;font:700 14px inherit;color:${G}">→ ${f.win}</div>`);
  return card;
}
```

# What it bought {#opt-scorecard}
Same outputs, leaner execution. Allocations fell everywhere; **time** moved only where allocations were the bottleneck.
```{ojs}
viewof _ = {
  const rows = [
    ["PCA",     "preallocate, @view, svd!, drop-sort",            "17×",   "1.2×",  "~flat"],
    ["PMD",     "preallocate, mul!, BLAS.ger!, alloc-free",        "7000×", "340×",  "1.5× faster"],
    ["PLS",     "preallocate, mul!, ger!, @view, lazy transpose",  "8×",    "~flat", "~flat"],
    ["JIVE",    "preallocate, fused mul!, svd!, algebraic (I−VVᵀ)","3.7×",  "3.1×",  "29% faster"],
    ["sPLS-DA", "preallocate, mul!, ger!, in-place bcast, sortperm!","6×",  "3×",    "12% faster"]
  ];
  const head = ["Function","Key tricks","Allocs ↓","Mem ↓","Time"];
  const O="#E78824", G="#4D701F", ink="#2a2a2a";
  const tbl = html`<table style="border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:15px;font-family:inherit"></table>`;

  // header
  const thead = html`<thead></thead>`;
  const hrow = html`<tr></tr>`;
  head.forEach((h,i)=>{
    const th = html`<th>${h}</th>`;
    th.style.cssText = `text-align:${i<2?'left':'center'};padding:7px 10px;border-bottom:2px solid ${O};color:${ink};font-weight:800`;
    hrow.append(th);
  });
  thead.append(hrow);
  tbl.append(thead);

  // body
  const tb = html`<tbody></tbody>`;
  rows.forEach((r,ri)=>{
    const tr = html`<tr></tr>`;
    tr.style.background = ri%2 ? "#faf8f5" : "#ffffff";
    r.forEach((c,ci)=>{
      const td = html`<td>${c}</td>`;
      const speed = ci>=2 && ci<=4 && /×|faster/.test(c);
      td.style.cssText = `padding:6px 10px;text-align:${ci<2?'left':'center'};border-bottom:1px solid #eee;`+
        (ci===0?`font-weight:800;color:${G};`:``)+
        (speed?`font-weight:700;color:${O};`:``);
      tr.append(td);
    });
    tb.append(tr);
  });
  tbl.append(tb);

  return tbl;
}
```

<br>

<div class="uthsc-green-box" style="font-size:0.82em; padding:8px 14px;">
Biggest single win: **JIVE's** $I-VV^{\top}$ projector. Forming the $n\times n$ matrix was the memory blowup — rewriting it as `tmp .-= (tmp*V)*V'` (same result, small intermediates) cut **871 → 277 MiB**.
</div>


# Part 3 — Sparse PLS-DA {#splsda}

Classify samples *and* pick the handful of variables that matter — in one model.

# Sparse PLS-DA: the idea {#splsda-why}

You have **many variables, few samples** (thousands of genes, dozens of patients) and a **class label** per patient. sPLS-DA separates the classes with a *weighted combination of genes* — each gene's **weight** (its *loading*) says how much it matters — and an **L1 penalty** forces most weights to exactly **0**, leaving a short, named gene list.

*Drag the slider: the penalty keeps only the strongest genes and zeroes the rest.*

```{ojs}
viewof keepX = Inputs.range([1, 50], {step: 1, value: 8, label: "Genes to keep (sparsity)"})
```

```{ojs}
splsdaWhy = {
  function mul(a){return function(){a|=0;a=a+0x6D2B79F5|0;let t=Math.imul(a^a>>>15,1|a);t=t+Math.imul(t^t>>>7,61|t)^t;return((t^t>>>14)>>>0)/4294967296;};}
  const rng=mul(9); const randn=()=>{let u=0,v=0;while(!u)u=rng();while(!v)v=rng();return Math.sqrt(-2*Math.log(u))*Math.cos(2*Math.PI*v);};

  const p=50;
  const truth = d3.range(p).map(i => i<10 ? (0.8+0.5*rng())*(i%2?-1:1) : 0);
  const raw   = truth.map(t => t + 0.28*randn());            // fixed weights, never change
  const order = d3.range(p).sort((a,b)=>Math.abs(raw[b])-Math.abs(raw[a]));
  const keepSet = new Set(order.slice(0, keepX));            // top-keepX survive

  const W=900,H=250,padL=46,padT=34,padB=34;
  const m=d3.max(raw,d=>Math.abs(d))*1.1;
  const O="#E78824", Od="#C14C23", grey="#dcdcdc";
  const svg=d3.create("svg").attr("viewBox",`0 0 ${W} ${H}`)
    .style("width","100%").style("max-width","880px").style("height","auto").style("display","block").style("margin","0 auto");

  const x=d3.scaleBand().domain(d3.range(p)).range([padL,W-16]).padding(0.3);
  const y=d3.scaleLinear().domain([-m,m]).range([H-padB,padT]);

  svg.append("text").attr("transform",`translate(14,${(padT+H-padB)/2}) rotate(-90)`)
     .attr("text-anchor","middle").attr("font-size",11).attr("fill","#777").text("gene weight");
  svg.append("line").attr("x1",padL).attr("x2",W-16).attr("y1",y(0)).attr("y2",y(0)).attr("stroke","#ccc");

  // bars are ALWAYS drawn at their true height; only the FILL changes with keepX
  svg.append("g").selectAll("rect").data(d3.range(p)).join("rect")
    .attr("x",i=>x(i)).attr("width",x.bandwidth())
    .attr("y",i=>Math.min(y(0),y(raw[i])))
    .attr("height",i=>Math.abs(y(raw[i])-y(0))+0.6)
    .attr("fill",i=> keepSet.has(i) ? O : grey)
    .style("transition","fill 0.25s ease");

  const kept = Math.min(keepX,p);
  svg.append("text").attr("x",W-16).attr("y",padT-12).attr("text-anchor","end")
     .attr("font-size",14).attr("font-weight",800).attr("fill",Od)
     .text(`${kept} of ${p} genes active`);
  svg.append("text").attr("x",(padL+W-16)/2).attr("y",H-6).attr("text-anchor","middle")
     .attr("font-size",11.5).attr("fill","#777").text("orange = kept by the penalty · grey = dropped (weight → 0)");
  return svg.node();
}
```

# The math {#splsda-math}

- $X$ ($n\times p$) — data: $n$ samples × $p$ genes (centered/scaled)
- $Y$ ($n\times k$) — labels as a 0/1 indicator, one column per class
- $u$ ($p$) — **gene loadings**, one weight per gene (made sparse)
- $v$ ($k$) — **class loadings**, one weight per class

**The goal** — choose $u,v$ so the weighted genes and weighted classes agree as strongly as possible, using only a few genes:

$$\max_{u,\,v}\ \underbrace{u^{\top}X^{\top}Y\,v}_{X\text{–}Y\text{ agreement}}\quad\text{s.t.}\quad \|u\|_2=\|v\|_2=1,\ \ \underbrace{\|u\|_0\le \texttt{keepX}}_{\le\,\text{keepX genes}}$$

- Scores: $t_X = Xu,\quad t_Y = Yv$
- Sparsity via **soft-thresholding** $S(a,\lambda)=\operatorname{sign}(a)(|a|-\lambda)_+$, with $\lambda$ chosen so exactly `keepX` weights survive


<!--
# The algorithm {#splsda-algo}

For each component, alternate two updates until the loadings settle — then deflate and move to the next.

```{ojs}
viewof sdaStep = {
  const labels=["① Initialize","② Update u (sparse)","③ Update v","④ Converged?","⑤ Deflate X"];
  const wrap=html`<div style="display:flex;gap:8px;flex-wrap:wrap;margin-bottom:14px"></div>`;
  const els=labels.map((t,k)=>{ const b=html`<button>${t}</button>`;
    b.style.cssText="font:700 13px inherit;padding:6px 13px;border-radius:9px;border:2px solid #cfcfcf;background:#fbfbfa;color:#2a2a2a;cursor:pointer";
    b.onclick=()=>{wrap.value=k;render();wrap.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input"));}; return b; });
  els.forEach(e=>wrap.append(e));
  const render=()=>els.forEach((b,k)=>{ const on=k===wrap.value;
    b.style.borderColor=on?"#C14C23":"#cfcfcf"; b.style.background=on?"#fff2e6":"#fbfbfa"; b.style.color=on?"#C14C23":"#2a2a2a"; });
  wrap.value=0; render(); return wrap;
}
```

```{ojs}
sdaView = {
  const O="#E78824",Od="#C14C23",G="#4D701F",ink="#2a2a2a";
  const frames=[
    {t:"Initialize from the cross-block SVD",
     b:"Each component starts at the leading singular vectors of XᵀY — the ordinary (dense) PLS direction, the strongest covariance link between the variables and the class indicator, before any sparsity is imposed.",
     m:"u,\\,v \\;\\leftarrow\\; \\text{top singular vectors of } X^{\\top}Y"},
    {t:"Update the sparse X-loading u",
     b:"Carry the current Y-score back onto X to get a fresh X-weight, soft-threshold it so only keepX variables survive, then renormalize. This single step is where variable selection happens.",
     m:"u \\;=\\; \\text{normalize}\\big(\\,S_\\lambda(X^{\\top}t_Y)\\,\\big),\\qquad S_\\lambda(a)=\\operatorname{sign}(a)\\,(|a|-\\lambda)_+"},
    {t:"Update the Y-loading v",
     b:"Carry the new X-score onto Y and renormalize. The class (Y) side gets no penalty — every class column is allowed to contribute.",
     m:"t_X = Xu,\\qquad v = \\text{normalize}\\big(Y^{\\top}t_X\\big)"},
    {t:"Check convergence",
     b:"Compare each loading to the previous pass. When both have essentially stopped moving (or a max-iteration cap is hit), the component is done.",
     m:"\\max\\big(\\lVert u-u_{\\text{old}}\\rVert^2,\\ \\lVert v-v_{\\text{old}}\\rVert^2\\big) \\;<\\; \\text{tol}"},
    {t:"Store scores, then deflate X",
     b:"Save this component's scores and loadings, then regress X on its own score and subtract that rank-1 piece, so the next component is forced to find new structure. Y is left untouched — that is the discriminant (DA) mode.",
     m:"p_X = \\frac{X^{\\top}t_X}{t_X^{\\top}t_X},\\qquad X \\leftarrow X - t_X\\,p_X^{\\top}"}
  ];
  const f=frames[sdaStep];
  const card=html`<div style="border:2px solid #eee;border-top:5px solid ${O};border-radius:14px;padding:16px 22px;background:#fff;min-height:230px"></div>`;
  card.append(html`<div style="font:800 21px inherit;color:${Od};margin-bottom:6px">${f.t}</div>`);
  card.append(html`<div style="font:400 16px inherit;line-height:1.45;color:${ink};margin-bottom:16px">${f.b}</div>`);
  const eq=html`<div style="text-align:center;font-size:19px;color:${ink}">${tex.block`${f.m}`}</div>`;
  card.append(eq);
  if(sdaStep===4) card.append(html`<div style="margin-top:14px;font:600 13px inherit;color:#777">↩ loop back to ① for the next component</div>`);
  return card;
}
```
-->






# {data-background-image="assets/uthsc-building-faded.png" data-background-size="cover" data-background-position="center" data-background-color="#ffffff"}

<div class="thankyou-slide">

<div class="thankyou-content">

Thank you for your attention!

</div>

</div>
